name: Hummus-Couscous Loaf
recipeIngredient: 1 small Eggplant, cut into french-fry sized strips
2 Carrots, chopped into very small pieces (minced)
1 1/2 cups Vegetable broth, or 1 1/2 cups water mixed with 1 tablespoon vegetable broth seasoning
2 tablespoons Egg replacer or potato starch
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. In a saucepan, add the couscous and water. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and cover. Cook until all the water is gone, about 10 minutes. Turn off the heat.
3. While the couscous is cooking, saute the vegetables in a large saucepan. Spray with olive oil or brush with a pastry brush. Add the water and heat over moderate heat until the water starts to simmer. Add the eggplant strips, carrots, garlic, salt, and pepper. Saute until soft, adding a little more water if too dry. Turn off the heat.
4. In a mixing bowl or blender, mix the chickpeas, tahini paste, lemon juice, and vegetable broth. Blend into a smooth paste using a hand blender or blender. Add the egg replacer or potato starch, and the flour. Blend until smooth.
5. With the heat turned off on the large saucepan, add the chickpea (hummus) mixture to the vegetables and coat well. Then add the couscous and mix the vegetable/hummus mixture into the couscous.
6. Lightly grease a glass bread loaf pan with olive oil. Add the couscous/vegetable/hummus mixture. Press into the loaf pan. Dust the top of the loaf with paprika and black pepper.
7. Bake for 50-55 minutes. Let stand 10-15 minutes before serving. Serve in thick slices with a green vegetable such as cooked spinach, a salad such as lettuce topped with fresh orange slices, and if desired, a bread such as warm whole-wheat pita bread.
